resources.string: Please select a valid .ico or .png file.
Cannot write to the selected folder
Sorry, your device may not be supported
Uninstall %1$s and permanently delete its installed game folder?
Remove %1$s from your library. Game files on disk will stay untouched?
No games installed. Use a Store tab or the + button to add games.
No Games Installed\nClick to open Winlator Backend
Select an exe and provide a name
Sign into a store or add a custom game to build your library.
Use a single folder across all stores
You\'ll need to sign in again to access your library.
A default download path has already been configured in Settings > Stores. If you wish to set a unique custom path for this specific installation, click Proceed.
Please sign in to access the %1$s store.
Do you want to remove this container?
Do you want to duplicate this container?
Choose an action for this container backup.
No container backups were found in Google Drive.
Do you want to remove this shortcut?
This shortcut is no longer available. Please delete or recreate it.
Failed to remove the shortcut. Please try again.
ColdClient is the preferred way to handle Steam emulation and DRM. It patches DRM at runtime without modifying game files.
Strip SteamStub DRM from the exe on disk. Only enable if the game fails to launch without it. ColdClient\'s runtime patcher handles most games automatically.
Only enable if DLCs are not detected or saves with DLC are not working
Launches the real Steam client in Wine\nRequires a Steam account and may reduce performance\nNot all games work
Reset this game to use the selected container defaults?
Per-game settings reset to selected container defaults
Do you want to remove this profile?
Do you want to duplicate this profile?
Do you want to download the selected profiles?
Adjust the transparency of the on-screen controls.
Enable DInput for Games in Wine
Enable XInput for Games in Wine

If your game utilizes the XInput API for game controller support, please enable this option.

This option will affect both virtual (provided by Input Controls) and physical game controllers.

If your game utilizes the DInput API for game controller support, please enable this option.

This option will affect both virtual (provided by Input Controls) and physical game controllers.
Some games may support both APIs. Enabling both XInput and DInput simultaneously in such games may cause control issues, as seen in titles like The Binding of Isaac and the Resident Evil series.
Emulator is determined by wine version. ARM64EC uses FEXCore, x86_64 uses Box64.
Restart container for changes to take effect

WineD3D is a translation layer for DirectX 1-11 to OpenGL.

DXVK is a translation layer for Direct3D 9-11 to Vulkan.

VKD3D is a translation layer for Direct3D 12 API to Vulkan.

CNC DDraw is an implementation of DirectDraw for better compatibility with classic games.
Wine/Proton package \'%1$s\' is not installed and could not be downloaded.
No Wine or Proton installed. Install one first.

Handling of flags on CALL/RET opcodes

0 : Treats CALL/RET as if it never needed flags (faster, unstable)
1 : Most RET will need flags, most CALLS will not
2 : All CALL/RET will need flags (slower)
Use of Float/Double for x87 emulation

Dynarec building BigBlock

0 : Don\'t try to build block as big as possible
1 : Build Dynarec block as big as possible
2 : Build Dynarec block bigger (continue when block overlaps, but only for blocks in elf memory)
3 : Build Dynarec block bigger (continue when block overlaps, for all types of memory)

Simulation of Strong Memory model

0 : Don\'t try anything special
1 : Enable some Memory Barrier when writing to memory (on some MOV opcode)
2 : All 1 plus a Memory Barrier on every write to memory using MOV
3 : All 2 plus Memory Barrier when reading from memory and on some SSE/SSE2 opcodes
Defines the max allowed forward value when building Block
Defines whether or not Dynarec will wait for the FillBlock to be ready

Tweak memory barriers to reduce performance impact of strong memory emulation

0 : Use regular safe barriers
1 : Use weak barriers for mild performance boost
2 : Use weak barriers, additionally disable last write barriers

Generate aligned atomics only (only available on Arm64 for now).

0 : Generate unaligned atomics handling code.
1 : Generate aligned atomics only, which is faster and smaller code size, but will cause SIGBUS for LOCK prefixed opcodes operating on aligned data addresses.

Enable or disable the use of deferred flags.

0 : Disable the use of deferred flags.
1 : Enable the use of deferred flags.

Allow continue running a block that is unprotected and potentially dirty.

0 : Do not allow continue running a block that is unprotected and potentially dirty.
1 : Allow continue to run a dynablock that write data in the same page as code. It can gets faster in loading time of some game but can also get unexpected crashes.
2 : Will also, when it detect an HotPage, flag that page as NEVERCLEAN, and so it will not be write protected but Block build from that page will always be tested. It can be faster that way (but some SMC case might not be trapped).

Enable or disable the use of native flags.

0 : Do not use native flags.
1 : Use native flags when possible.

Enable x86 PAUSE emulation, may help the performance of spinlocks.

0 : Ignore x86 PAUSE instruction.
1 : Use YIELD to emulate x86 PAUSE instruction.
2 : Use WFI to emulate x86 PAUSE instruction.
3 : Use SEVL+WFE to emulate x86 PAUSE instruction.

AVX and AVX2 implemented, along with BMI1, BMI2, ADX, FMA, F16C and RDANDR extension!

0 : Disable AVX extension
1 : enable AVX, BMI1, F16C and VAES extension.
2 : All 1 plus enable AVX2, BMI2, FMA, ADX,VPCLMULQDQ and RDRAND.
\
Maximum number of CPUs presented to programs by Box64
Detect UnityPlayer.dll and apply strongmem settings
Force every memory allocation in 32bit address spaces
Enables TSO IR ops (required for multithreaded apps).
Makes vector load/stores atomic when TSO is enabled.
Uses half-barrier atomics for unaligned loads/stores under TSO.
Makes REP MOVS / REP STOS atomic under TSO.
Enables multiblock code compilation. Can cause longer JIT compilation and stutter.
Uses volatile metadata from PE files for TSO when available.
Special SMC + JIT block hacks for Mono detection.
Hides CPUID hypervisor bit (useful for apps that crash with it).
Disables FEXCore JIT L2 cache lookup, saving memory but introducing stutters.
Switches FEXCore JIT L1 cache to be dynamically sized, saving memory but introducing stutters.
Emulates X87 floating point using 64-bit precision. This reduces emulation accuracy and may result in rendering bugs.
Do you want to duplicate this preset?
Do you want to remove this preset?
Changes save automatically while editing this custom preset.
Built-in presets are read-only. Create or duplicate a preset to customize these variables.

Here you can set value of extra-keys variable like in Termux app (read this) and more 
 E.g. META for META key, 
 KEYBOARD for soft keyboard toggling key, 
 PREFERENCES for preferences opening key, 
 MOUSE_HELPER for mouse helper toggling key, 
 STYLUS_HELPER for stylus click override overlay toggling key, 
 EXIT for application exit key.
Disabling may fix graphical glitches on some devices
Native rendering enabled (takes effect on next launch)
Native rendering disabled (takes effect on next launch)
WinNative is running, do not kill or swipe this notification
Color, CRT, FXAA and shader options
Shrink the game into a floating window
Do you want to end this process?
Do you want to add a new profile?
Do you want to remove this controller?
Press any button on your controller

Depends on your Physical Controller:

• Is Axis — trigger has a linear range of motion
• Is Button — trigger acts as a simple switch

Note: using Is Button with a linear trigger loses analog data.
Press any button on the controller for
Also apply turbo to L2/R2 as digital presses
Do you want to overwrite the current profile or save as a new profile?
This option will take effect at the next startup.
Do you want to reinstall imagefs? This operation will restore system files, your containers won\'t be changed.
Set display refresh rate and DXVK frame limit
Manage where Winlator stores its working data and where exported shortcuts are written.
Adjust pointer speed for touch, trackpad, and mouse input inside the XServer session.
Keeps direct mouse capture enabled for games that need raw pointer input. Disable it with Volume Down.
Turns off XInput handling when you want exclusive mouse and keyboard control.
Expose Winlator files to Android document pickers and file managers.
Open supported web links in the Android browser instead of inside Wine.
Allow copy and paste between Android and Wine sessions.
Restore bundled system files without removing your existing containers.
Automatically check for new app versions on launch and periodically.
Do you want to remove this soundfont?
Soundfont you want to install is already exist.
Install, remove, and choose the SoundFont used for MIDI playback.
Write Wine debug output to file
Write Box86 & Box64 debug output to file
Write FEXCore debug output to file
Log Steam authentication and session events for debugging.
Choose which Wine channels are included when Wine debug logging is enabled.
Log gamepad, touch, and keyboard input events
Log download resolver and WinNative fallback events
No debug logs available. Enable debugging options first and launch a game.
Log WinNative process errors, warnings and crashes to file
Tap to toggle channels. Enabled channels are included in Wine debug output.
Please keep the app open while this finishes.
Install a package or switch type to get started.
The Windows compatibility layer used to run games and apps. You need at least one Wine or FEXCore version installed.
Valve\'s Wine-based build with extra patches for games. Useful for some titles, but standard Wine usually works fine.
Translates DirectX 9, 10, and 11 to Vulkan. This usually improves performance in older Windows games.
Translates DirectX 12 to Vulkan. Needed for many newer Windows games.
Translates x86 / x64 Windows code so it can run on ARM devices. Box64 or FEXCore is required for most games.
A Box64 build for Wine WoW64 mode. Only needed when using a 64-bit prefix with 32-bit app support.
An alternative x86 / x64 translator. Try it if a game has issues or poor performance with Box64.

Please uninstall the conflicting content before downloading this package.
Installed path: %1$s
Warning: The additional content contains untrusted files. Generally, the following files should not be modified. Please decide whether to proceed with the installation.
Do you want to remove this content?
Unable to remove this content, because a container is currently using this Wine.
None downloaded. Please download one in Components.
Do you want to remove this driver?
No downloadable ZIP assets were found in the fetched releases.
System image not installed. Please install components first.
Recommended Components downloads and installs the supported DXVK, VKD3D, FEX, Box64, and Wowbox64 packages automatically.
Each button downloads the package, installs it, and creates its default container automatically.
Choose which components to install. Wine/Proton will auto-create a container.
No components available for this category.
Open each container and set the defaults you want every new game to inherit. New games will target the x86-64 container by default.
Store sign-in is optional. Each sign-in returns here when it finishes.
No Wine or Proton packages installed yet.
Install a Wine or Proton package from the previous step, or skip this for now.
Open Steam app → tap the QR icon
Approve this login from\nyour Steam mobile app
Working on your store login sync\u2026
Sign in to Google Play Games to sync store logins.
Google Play Games sign-in is still finishing. Wait a moment and try cloud sync again.
Google Play Games connected. Restore is available for %1$s.
Google Play Games connected. Tap Backup to save %1$s logins.
Google Play Games connected. Backup and Restore are ready.
Google Play Games sync disabled for this app.
Sign in to Google Play Games first.
No saved store logins were found in Google cloud sync.
Cloud backup already matches the current store logins.
No local store logins are available to back up.
Store logins restored and synced for %1$s.
No store logins available to sync yet.
Sign in to back up %1$s login tokens.
No backed up store logins found yet.
Local and cloud store logins differ. Use Backup to overwrite cloud data or Restore to pull the saved tokens.
Local store logins are ready to back up for %1$s.
Cloud login restore is available for %1$s.
Store logins are synced with Google Play Games.
Store login sync ran into a problem.
Google Play Games needs to finish authentication before cloud sync can access Saved Games. Open the Play Games profile prompt if shown, then try again.
Google Play Games Saved Games rejected this build. Check the Play Games app ID, linked package name, and signing certificate in Play Console.
Google account is connected, but Google Play Games Saved Games rejected this build. Check the Play Games app ID, linked package, and signing certificate.
Google cloud sync failed because the network request did not complete or Google Play services needs attention.
No saved store logins were found in Google cloud sync yet.
Google cloud sync failed before Google Saved Games could open.
Automatically back up game saves to Google Drive each time you exit a game.
Download cloud saves on launch and sync/backup them on exit.
Launch this game without cloud save download or exit sync.
Backing up save to Google Drive…
